data.title: Bird Construction outlines path to 8% EBITDA margin by 2027 amid record $10B backlog and strategic acquisitions
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Bird Construction Inc. (BDT:CA) Q3 2025

Management View

  • Terrance McKibbon, President and CEO, opened the call highlighting Bird's recognition on the 2025 TSX 30, ranking 17th, and emphasized, "Being recognized among the top 30 performing companies on the TSX underscores the success of our strategic focus, strong execution and disciplined balanced approach to capital allocation that positions Bird for continued profitable growth in today's active market."
  • McKibbon outlined the impact of the FRPD acquisition, stating it "expands Bird's natural infrastructure presence adding marine construction, dredging and land foundation capabilities to our full-service civil platform" and creates "meaningful cross-selling opportunities across our businesses."
  • The CEO noted strong demand in key sectors, especially in industrial, infrastructure, and buildings, with year-to-date securements exceeding $3.8 billion and a record combined backlog surpassing $10 billion.
  • Wayne Gingrich, CFO, reported, "Construction revenue for the third quarter of $951.4 million represented a 5.8% increase compared to the same period in 2024. Over 60% of the growth was organic, with continued strength in work programs for mining clients in the East Harbour Transit Hub driving infrastructure growth and higher institutional construction in Eastern Canada driving buildings growth."
  • Gingrich called attention to a unique credit risk: "Subsequent to quarter end, the company became aware of circumstances that arose after the end of the quarter that led us to be concerned about the creditworthiness of one of our customers...we expect the maximum exposure to be approximately $62 million."

Outlook

  • Management reaffirmed confidence in long-term targets, with McKibbon stating, "We remain confident in the trajectory towards our 2027 strategic plan targets, reinforcing Bird's position as a trusted partner in delivering Canada's critical infrastructure."
  • Guidance suggests fourth quarter revenue will be lower than the prior year, attributed to industrial projects shifting into 2026, but the underlying margin profile of the backlog remains strong.
  • Gingrich explained, "If you think about it, if we got 140 basis points to close between now and in the end of '27 when we think we're going to get to 8% EBITDA...we feel pretty confident in getting to 8%."

Financial Results

  • Revenue for Q3 2025 reached $951.4 million, up 5.8% year-over-year, with organic growth contributing over 60%.
  • Gross profit was $101.9 million with a margin of 10.7%, down from $102.3 million and 11.4% in Q3 2024.
  • Adjusted EBITDA was $66.9 million, margin 7%. Net income and earnings per share were $31.7 million and $0.57, compared to $36.2 million and $0.66 in 2024.
  • Year-to-date revenue increased 2.4% to $2.52 billion, gross profit grew 11.7% to $259.5 million, and adjusted EBITDA rose 10.7% to $155.9 million.
  • Free cash flow conversion of net income was 27.4%. The company held $113.9 million in cash and $281.7 million available under its credit facility at quarter end.

Q&A

  • Krista Friesen, CIBC, asked about the margin improvement drivers for 2027 targets. Gingrich responded, "It's a couple of things that close that gap...volumes are obviously down this year. So we are going to get leverage on our cost structure going forward...And then the other thing, especially with an example of Jacob Brothers or FRPD, we are going to get growth in our infrastructure side as well, and that's a very high-margin business for us."
  • Chris Murray, ATB Capital, pressed for confidence in the timing of project ramp-ups. McKibbon replied, "There's going to be an inflection point at some point where this really starts to accelerate next year," and confirmed Q4 revenue expectations are inclusive of FRPD.
  • Murray also inquired about the $62 million customer credit risk. Gingrich stated, "We disclosed the full potential risk, that's out there. That's a $62 million combined in contract assets and accounts receivable...we are going to take a provision in fourth quarter on this based on what we think we can recover."
  • Maxim Sytchev, NBF, asked about maintenance work delays. McKibbon explained, "The bulk of the pressure on our maintenance business was centered in oil and gas. And I think those clients just decided to delay their large maintenance turnaround...we expect that, that scope will come back in '26."
  • Michael Tupholme, TD Cowen, sought clarity on capacity and talent. McKibbon replied, "We're extremely careful when we're pursuing something that we've got a team assembled for. And that's putting pressure on our earnings in 2025 because we've got these teams assembled ready to go."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts' tone was cautious but constructive, focusing on margin trajectory, credit risk, and project delays, with repeated requests for additional detail and reassurance.
  • Management maintained a confident and measured tone, frequently referencing strong backlog, embedded margins, and disciplined execution. Phrases such as "we are confident" and "highly confident" appeared throughout, especially when discussing long-term targets and recovery of delayed work.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management’s sentiment remains optimistic, but there is increased emphasis on near-term uncertainties and risks, notably the unique customer credit event.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The current quarter features a substantial increase in backlog, now exceeding $10 billion compared to $4.6 billion contracted and $3.8 billion pending as of Q2.
  • Q3 revenue grew to $951.4 million from $850.8 million in Q2, with higher organic contribution.
  • Gross profit and margin dipped slightly quarter-over-quarter, while adjusted EBITDA margin also saw a minor decrease.
  • Guidance is more tempered for Q4, with explicit mention of revenue headwinds due to project delays, whereas Q2 guidance was more optimistic about the second half.
  • Analysts in both quarters pressed for clarity on project timing and margin outlook, but this quarter, their focus also included the $62 million credit risk.
  • Management’s confidence in reaching 2027 targets remains high, but language around near-term uncertainty and risks has increased.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management disclosed a potential $62 million exposure due to creditworthiness concerns with a single customer, with resolution and potential impairment expected in Q4.
  • Project start delays and deferrals, especially in industrial and maintenance, are impacting near-term revenue and margin progression.
  • Analysts focused on the risk of further delays and the impact of customer credit events on financials.
  • Management is actively pursuing recovery on the credit issue and emphasizes that it is a unique and isolated event.

Final Takeaway

Bird Construction delivered resilient Q3 results with robust revenue growth and a record $10 billion backlog, underpinned by significant new awards and strategic acquisitions such as FRPD. While near-term revenue and margin progression will be affected by project delays and a notable customer credit risk, management remains highly confident in achieving the 8% adjusted EBITDA margin target by 2027, supported by disciplined execution, collaborative contract models, and a diversified, high-margin backlog.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: U.S., Argentina to open markets to key products in trade framework
data.description:
The U.S. and Argentina agreed to a framework, under which the countries will open their markets for key products, in a move to deepen bilateral trade and investment cooperation, the White House said in a statement on Thursday.

Argentina will provide preferential market access for U.S. goods exports, including certain medicines, chemicals, machinery, information technologies, medical devices, motor vehicles, and a wide range of agricultural products.

The agreement provides the struggling Argentine economy with a needed boost, while U.S. agricultural products will be allowed to flow into the country. U.S. farmers and ranchers have been among those most hurt by the intensified trade tensions since President Donald Trump's second term began.

The U.S. will remove what President Donald Trump calls "reciprocal tariffs" on some unavailable natural resources and non-patented articles for use in pharmaceutical applications, the statement said. In addition, the countries committed to improve market access conditions for trade in beef.

Argentina has dismantled many non-tariff barriers that restricted access to its market and will phase out the statistical tax for U.S. goods, it said.

The South American country has opened its market to live cattle from the U.S. and has committed to allow market access for U.S. poultry within a year.

"The United States and Argentina will work expeditiously to finalize the text of the Agreement for signature and undertake their respective domestic formalities in advance of the Agreement entering into force," the White House's statement said.

Global X MSCI Argentina ETF (ARGT)  gained 0.6% in Thursday after-hours trading. Meanwhile, the Argentinian peso (ARS:USD) only ticked up 0.02% against the U.S. dollar.

data.title: Figure Technology stock spikes after Q3 revenue surpasses consensus
data.description:
Figure Technology Solutions (FIGR) shares climbed 10% in Thursday after-hours trading after the blockchain-based lender turned in Q3 revenue that easily beat the Wall Street consensus, marking its first quarter as a public company.

"The continued success of Figure’s marketplace approach to tokenized consumer loan origination drove significant growth in earnings" on a Y/Y basis, CEO  Michael Tannenbaum said in a statement.

Q3 adjusted net revenue of $156.4M, exceeding the $119.4M average analyst estimate, advanced 42% from the year-earlier period. Net income of $90M surged 227% Y/Y.

Consumer Loan Marketplace volume rose 70% from a year ago to $2.5B in Q3. That includes Figure Connect volume of $1.1B, up from $767M in Q2), reaching 46% of CLM volume in the three-month period.

Volume from new product categories topped $80M in Q3, including crypto-backed loans, small/medium business loans, debt service coverage ratio loans, and HELOC for seniors interest-only mortgage loans.

Adjusted EBITDA rose to $86.4M in Q3 from $49.4M in Q3 2024. Adjusted EBITDA margin of 55.4% vs. 44.9% in Q3 2024.

Conference call on Nov. 14 at 8:30 a.m. ET.

data.title: Freehold Royalties reports Q3 results
data.description:
• Freehold Royalties press release (FRHLF): Q3 $59 million in funds from operations ($0.36/share)
  • Revenue of $74M.
  • $44 million in dividends paid ($0.27/share)(3);
  • 16,054 boe/d(4) of total production, a 10% increase from the third quarter of 2024;
  • 10,477 bbls/d of total crude oil and natural gas liquids production, a 12% increase from the third quarter 2024;
  • 65% liquids weighting, an increase from 64% in the third quarter of 2024;
  • Gross drilling of 282 wells, comprised of 83 wells in Canada and 199 in the U.S. similar levels to the third quarter 2024;
  • Continued active leasing program with 34 new leases signed (27 in Canada; 7 in the U.S.) contributing revenue of $1.7 million and $7.6 million for the first three quarters of 2025; and
  • $48.92/boe average realized price ($56.54/boe in the U.S. and $42.44/boe in Canada);
    • A 33% pricing premium on Freehold’s U.S. production reflecting higher liquids weighting, higher quality crude oil and reduced transportation costs.

data.title: Fennec Pharmaceuticals announces proposed offering of common shares
data.description:
• Fennec Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:FENC) stated on Thursday that it would sell its common shares in an underwritten registered public offering.
  • The company intends to grant the underwriters a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional 15% of its common shares sold in the public offering.
  • The company plans to use the net proceeds to repurchase and redeem certain indebtedness and the remaining net proceeds for working capital and general corporate purposes.
  • Piper Sandler & Co. and Craig-Hallum Capital Group LLC are acting as the joint book-running managers for the proposed public offering.

data.title: Hudson Technologies appoints Kenneth Gaglione as Chairman, President and CEO
data.description:
•  Hudson Technologies (NASDAQ:HDSN) announced on Thursday the appointment of Kenneth Gaglione as Chairman of the Board, President, and CEO, effective November 24, 2025.
  • Gaglione brings in-depth experience in the refrigerants and specialty chemicals industries, along with a reliable track record of operational excellence and strategic growth leadership. He successfully led multiple strategic production initiatives and advanced the company’s supply chain and planning capabilities.
